message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Adding transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py; need to touch its tests too. What are you working on / which files do you need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My task: add strip_metadata param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. This likely touches the same function you're working on if your feature also involves exif_transpose. Please tell me what your feature is and which exact files/functions you need so we can split non-overlapping regions or sequence our edits.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: We both need the same two files since both features add a new keyword param to exif_transpose. Since true disjoint ownership isn't possible, propose sequencing instead: I'll implement strip_metadata now (small, self-contained addition at the end of exif_transpose), send DONE when finished, then you implement transform_callback on top of my changes (re-read the file first so you don't clobber my edit). Reply ACCEPT if that order works for you, or REVISE if you'd rather go first.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Same files unavoidable - both our features add a new keyword-only param to exif_transpose(). To minimize merge conflicts: (1) each of us only touches the lines strictly needed for our own param, no reformatting/reordering of existing code; (2) both append our new kwarg at the END of the signature (after existing in_place) rather than inserting in the middle; (3) I'll add 'transform_callback: Callable[[int], ...] | None = None' as the last param, you add yours similarly after mine - won't touch your logic. (4) In test file, we each only append new test functions, not edit each other's tests. Agree?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed. To keep it clean I'll wait for your DONE before touching ImageOps.py - you add transform_callback as the last param plus your logic, send DONE, then I'll add strip_metadata as the new last param (after yours) plus my logic, only appending new test functions to test_imageops.py, no edits to your lines/tests.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed - you go first with strip_metadata, send DONE when finished. I'll snapshot the file state at that point, implement transform_callback on top, and diff only against your finished state so my patch.txt contains just my feature. Waiting for your DONE now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Confirming: I'll go first per your latest message. Implementing transform_callback now as the last param, will only append new test functions, then send DONE.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished strip_metadata. In exif_transpose() I: (1) added 'strip_metadata: bool = False' as the last keyword-only param on both @overload signatures and the impl signature (after in_place); (2) at the end of the 'if method is not None:' branch, after the existing orientation-tag cleanup, added 'if strip_metadata: exif.clear(); exif_image.info = {}'; (3) replaced the old 'elif not in_place: return image.copy()' tail with an 'else:' block that computes exif_image (image if in_place, else a copy) and applies the same strip_metadata clearing when there's no orientation tag to transpose. Only appended two new test functions (test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata, test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata_in_place) after test_exif_transpose_in_place - did not touch any existing lines. Safe for you to add transform_callback as the next last param now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished transform_callback. Added it as the last kwarg in all 3 signatures (2 overloads + impl), added new import Callable, and appended 4 new test functions after test_exif_transpose_in_place (no existing lines touched). All 38 tests in test_imageops.py pass, black/ruff clean. Your turn - go ahead and add strip_metadata after my transform_callback param.
